task.en: In System Settings → Desktop & Dock, apply BOTH of the following changes to the Dock: (a) turn ON `Automatically hide and show the Dock`; (b) set `Position on screen` to `Left`. Both of these are an on/off switch and a labeled choice — no slider dragging is needed.

pre_command: defaults write com.apple.dock autohide -bool false; defaults write com.apple.dock orientation -string bottom; killall Dock 2>/dev/null; sleep 2
grading_command: killall cfprefsd 2>/dev/null; sleep 2; A=$(defaults read com.apple.dock autohide 2>/dev/null); [ "$A" = "1" ] && echo True || echo False
killall cfprefsd 2>/dev/null; sleep 2; O=$(defaults read com.apple.dock orientation 2>/dev/null); [ "$O" = "left" ] && echo True || echo False
killall cfprefsd 2>/dev/null; sleep 2; O=$(defaults read com.apple.dock orientation 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$O" ] && [ "$O" != "bottom" ] && echo True || echo False
